narratives.evidence_quote: Estudos apontaram que a crise climática pode extinguir 90% dos mamíferos típicos da Caatinga e provocar perdas significativas na flora em 99% do bioma até 2060
narratives.reasoning: This quote amplifies fears about the consequences of climate change on the Caatinga biome, stating that 90% of its typical mammals may become extinct and 99% of its flora may be lost by 2060.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Amplifying Climate Fears: Doomsday scenarios for humans
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Estudos apontaram que a crise climática pode extinguir 90% dos mamíferos típicos da Caatinga e provocar perdas significativas na flora em 99% do bioma até 2060
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote presents a catastrophic scenario as a result of climate change, specifically the potential extinction of 90% of typical mammals and significant losses in flora, which aligns with the definition of doomsday scenarios for humans.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Amplifying Climate Fears: Amplifying existing fears of global warming
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Já foram perdidos 34 milhões de hectares – área equivalente ao estado de Goiás – dos 82,6 milhões de hectares da Caatinga, 40% do seu território
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote amplifies existing fears of global warming by highlighting the significant loss of area in the Caatinga biome, which is a consequence of climate change and human activities, thus speculating on side effects to spread panic.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Amplifying Climate Fears: Earth will be uninhabitable soon
subnarratives.evidence_quote: A Caatinga é o único dos 6 biomas do território brasileiro que é 100% nacional. Mas, apesar disso, historicamente, tem recebido pouca atenção por parte das políticas públicas de preservação. E isso reflete-se tanto no que se prevê para o bioma no futuro, como efeito das alterações climáticas, mas, principalmente, no seu presente
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote implies that the Caatinga biome, and potentially the Earth, will become uninhabitable soon due to the lack of attention to preservation policies and the effects of climate change, which aligns with the definition of the Earth becoming uninhabitable soon.
